Let me analyze the source records against the action boundary.

Action Boundary: Should demo emergency access EA9-001 grant LEDGER-READ to user U9-001?

Required elements per Policy POL9-001:
1. Exact user - U9-001 (in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02)
2. Exact group - LEDGER-READ (in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02)
3. Exact ticket - T9-001 (in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02, B9-01-SRC-03)
4. Active time window - 10:00Z to 11:00Z (in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02)
5. Removal schedule - REM9-001 schedules removal at 11:00Z (B9-01-SRC-05)

Let me check each element:
- User U9-001: matches in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02, B9-01-SRC-04, B9-01-SRC-05
- Group LEDGER-READ: matches in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02, B9-01-SRC-05
- Ticket T9-001: matches in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02, B9-01-SRC-03
- Time window 10:00Z to 11:00Z: matches in B9-01-SRC-01, B9-01-SRC-02
- Removal schedule at 11:00Z: matches in B9-01-SRC-05

All required elements are present and consistent across source records. The alarm review ALM9-001 was closed at 09:56Z, which is before the window starts at 10:00Z.


The source records form a complete chain validating all required parameters for the emergency access action.
</think>
